pr.title: Add `pre-commit` to `reqs/dev.in` and update documentation, then format code
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Repository tooling and code-style PR: added pre-commit to developer requirements, limited the clang-format pre-commit hook to C/C++ source under src, updated contributor documentation with pre-commit usage, regenerated reqs/dev.txt, and applied formatting-only churn across many src files. The only discussion evidence is the PR body resolving #1543 and an automated decomp-dev report saying GALE01 had no changes.

key_files.role: pre-commit clang-format hook scope update: files ^src/.*\.(c|h|cpp)$
compiled developer requirements updated for pre-commit and dependencies
formatted/cleanup source file; removed one include in shown excerpt
formatted source file; largest stage-formatting hunk set
formatted source file; largest changed source file by line count

what_changed: Regenerated `reqs/dev.txt`, adding pre-commit 4.6.0 and dependencies such as cfgv, identify, nodeenv, pyyaml, and virtualenv; the generated header changed from Python 3.11/pip-compile --strip-extras to Python 3.12/pip-compile --output-file=reqs/dev.txt reqs/dev.in.
Updated `.pre-commit-config.yaml` so the clang-format hook applies only to files matching `^src/.*\.(c|h|cpp)$`.
Updated `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` to recommend running clang-format on all C code in `src` via pre-commit, with commands to install dev requirements, install the hook, and run hooks on all files.
Applied broad clang-format/code-style churn across 92 changed files, mostly under `src/melee` and `src/sysdolphin`; the changed-file metadata reports 3778 additions and 3374 deletions.
smart_moves: Scoped the pre-commit clang-format hook to `src` C/C++ sources instead of running it over every repository file.
Bundled the documentation update with the requirements update so contributors have an explicit install-and-run workflow for the new hook.
Formatted existing code in the same PR after adding the hook, reducing immediate future churn from enabling the formatter.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Treat large formatting-only PRs as code-style/tooling history, not as evidence of decomp intent, semantic cleanup, or matching tactics unless the diff or comments explicitly show semantic changes.

review_feedback.text: Metadata reports 0 review comments and 0 reviews for this slice.

follow_up_queries: decomp-dev bot GALE01 No changes PR 2463

notes: Strong evidence for PR identity, merged state, author, title, changed-file counts, documentation/tooling edits, and automated bot comment.
The raw diff is very large and the loaded excerpt is truncated; changed_files.jsonl was read directly to recover the complete changed-file list.
The slice contains no human review comments and no explicit author discussion beyond `Resolves #1543.`
No build, objdiff, or matching claim should be inferred from this PR. The bot comment says `No changes`, but the slice does not explain the exact check scope beyond GALE01.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Formatting-only PRs are weak decomp evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2463 is useful as evidence of repository formatting/tooling practice, but its mass source churn should not be treated as evidence for semantic decomp decisions, naming choices, or matching tactics without additional diff/comment support.

source_update_candidates.title: Use pre-commit/clang-format for C code under src
source_update_candidates.text: Contributor documentation in PR #2463 says clang-format should be run on all C code in `src`, with pre-commit as the recommended workflow; the pre-commit config scopes clang-format to `^src/.*\.(c|h|cpp)$`.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a repo-level style/workflow rule candidate, not a decomp tactic. Curator should decide whether to add it to standards or keep it as repository documentation history.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the mass formatting changes as evidence that any affected function, symbol, or subsystem was semantically changed.
Do not infer reviewer intent; the slice has no human reviews or review comments.
Do not infer matching or assembly tactics from line wrapping, include whitespace, or clang-format output.
